methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
IQM Quantum Computers is a pre-IPO quantum computing company founded by Jan Goetz that has raised €600M+. The quantum computing sector comprises 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable to IQM. The quantum computing peer set, while containing 7 same-sector companies, lacks sufficient clustering at IQM's stage and valuation tier. Specifically, zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-tier private companies are available in the dataset, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for meaningful median-based triangulation. An all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of quantum computing business models, maturity levels, and technology approaches across the public and private universe.

Without a credible peer-median anchor, this analysis cannot produce a defensible valuation range using the data provided. Completion of a robust IQM valuation would require either: expansion of the peer set to include adjacently positioned deep-tech hardware or software infrastructure companies; collection of transaction multiples from recent financing rounds within the quantum computing sector; or application of alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis or venture capital method calibration. The primary uncertainty is the absence of sufficient comparable transaction data to triangulate fair value at IQM's pre-IPO stage and capital scale.
